---
title: "SO_P013: N-tuple Independent Loading Principle"
subtitle: "Components in n-tuple pattern should be loaded and handled independently by aspect rather than by component"
chapter: "CH01"
category: "principle"
number: "SO_P013"
date-created: "2025-04-13"
date-modified: "2025-08-17"
author: "Claude"
type: "principle"
law: "Structure and Organization Law"
article: 
  number: 13
  title: "N-tuple Independent Loading Principle"
related_to:
  - "UI_P004: Component N-tuple Pattern"
  - "UI_P003: Sidebar Filtering Only"
  - "CH00.MP017: Separation of Concerns"
  - "CH00.MP016: Modularity"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: false
    code-tools: true
    number-sections: true
---

# Article Overview {#overview}

**Structure and Organization Law Article 13 (N-tuple Independent Loading Principle)**

Components in an n-tuple pattern should be loaded and handled independently, treated as distinct entities each serving a specific purpose rather than as a single combined unit. Loading should be organized by aspect rather than by component.

:::{.callout-important}
## Core Principle
Load components by aspect type (all UIs together, all Servers together) rather than loading all aspects of a single component, aligning with the application's execution flow.
:::

# Section 1: Theoretical Foundation {#rationale}

## Subsection 1: Aspect-Oriented Loading {#aspect-oriented-loading}

The n-tuple pattern (like UI-Server-Defaults-Filters pattern) divides functionality into distinct aspects:
- Each aspect serves a specific purpose
- Aspects are conceptually related but executionally independent
- Loading order should align with execution flow

## Subsection 2: Independence Advantages {#independence-advantages}

1. **Selective Loading**: Only necessary aspects can be loaded when needed (e.g., loading only UI/Server in view-only mode)
2. **Independent Testing**: Each aspect can be tested independently of others
3. **Clearer Dependency Flow**: Dependencies between aspects become explicit rather than implicit
4. **Parallel Processing**: Aspects can be executed in parallel when appropriate
5. **Substitutability**: Individual aspects can be replaced or modified without affecting others

# Section 2: Loading Patterns {#loading-patterns}

## Subsection 1: Loading by Aspect (Recommended) {#by-aspect}

```r
# ✅ GOOD: Loading by aspect type
# Step 1: Load all UI components
ui_files <- list.files(
  path = "components",
  pattern = "*UI\\.R$",
  recursive = TRUE,
  full.names = TRUE
)
lapply(ui_files, source)

# Step 2: Load all Server components
server_files <- list.files(
  path = "components",
  pattern = "*Server\\.R$",
  recursive = TRUE,
  full.names = TRUE
)
lapply(server_files, source)

# Step 3: Load all Defaults components
defaults_files <- list.files(
  path = "components",
  pattern = "*Defaults\\.R$",
  recursive = TRUE,
  full.names = TRUE
)
lapply(defaults_files, source)

# Step 4: Load all Filters components
filters_files <- list.files(
  path = "components",
  pattern = "*Filters\\.R$",
  recursive = TRUE,
  full.names = TRUE
)
lapply(filters_files, source)
```

## Subsection 2: Loading by Component (Not Recommended) {#by-component}

```r
# ❌ BAD: Loading all aspects of a component together
# This violates the aspect independence principle
source("components/sidebar/marketingChannel/marketingChannelUI.R")
source("components/sidebar/marketingChannel/marketingChannelServer.R")
source("components/sidebar/marketingChannel/marketingChannelFilters.R")
source("components/sidebar/marketingChannel/marketingChannelDefaults.R")

# Then loading next component...
source("components/sidebar/productCategory/productCategoryUI.R")
source("components/sidebar/productCategory/productCategoryServer.R")
# ...
```

# Section 3: Organization Structure {#organization-structure}

## Subsection 1: Directory Structure {#directory-structure}

```
components/
├── sidebar/
│   ├── sidebarUI.R
│   ├── sidebarServer.R
│   ├── marketingChannel/
│   │   ├── marketingChannelUI.R
│   │   ├── marketingChannelServer.R
│   │   ├── marketingChannelDefaults.R
│   │   └── marketingChannelFilters.R
│   └── productCategory/
│       ├── productCategoryUI.R
│       ├── productCategoryServer.R
│       ├── productCategoryDefaults.R
│       └── productCategoryFilters.R
├── main/
│   ├── dashboard/
│   │   ├── dashboardUI.R
│   │   ├── dashboardServer.R
│   │   └── dashboardDefaults.R
│   └── analysis/
│       ├── analysisUI.R
│       ├── analysisServer.R
│       └── analysisDefaults.R
```

## Subsection 2: Loading Order Strategy {#loading-order}

```r
# Define loading order
loading_order <- list(
  # 1. Infrastructure
  infrastructure = c("config", "utils", "database"),
  
  # 2. Defaults (load first as others may depend on them)
  defaults = "*Defaults.R",
  
  # 3. UI components (define interface)
  ui = "*UI.R",
  
  # 4. Server logic (handle interactions)
  server = "*Server.R",
  
  # 5. Filters (data processing)
  filters = "*Filters.R",
  
  # 6. Tests (if in development mode)
  test = if (dev_mode) "*Test.R" else NULL
)

# Execute loading
for (aspect in names(loading_order)) {
  pattern <- loading_order[[aspect]]
  if (!is.null(pattern)) {
    files <- list.files(
      path = "components",
      pattern = pattern,
      recursive = TRUE,
      full.names = TRUE
    )
    lapply(files, source)
    message(sprintf("Loaded %d %s files", length(files), aspect))
  }
}
```

# Section 4: Function Organization {#function-organization}

## Subsection 1: Naming Conventions {#naming-conventions}

```r
# UI functions follow UI patterns and return UI elements
marketingChannelUI <- function(id) {
  ns <- NS(id)
  tagList(
    selectInput(ns("channel"), "Marketing Channel", choices = NULL)
  )
}

productCategoryUI <- function(id) {
  ns <- NS(id)
  tagList(
    selectInput(ns("category"), "Product Category", choices = NULL)
  )
}

# Server functions follow server patterns and handle reactivity
marketingChannelServer <- function(id, data) {
  moduleServer(id, function(input, output, session) {
    # Reactive logic
  })
}

productCategoryServer <- function(id, data) {
  moduleServer(id, function(input, output, session) {
    # Reactive logic
  })
}

# Filter functions follow data processing patterns
marketingChannelFilters <- function(data, selection) {
  data %>%
    filter(channel %in% selection)
}

productCategoryFilters <- function(data, selection) {
  data %>%
    filter(category %in% selection)
}
```

## Subsection 2: Aspect Interface Definition {#aspect-interfaces}

```r
# Define clear interfaces for each aspect
# UI aspect interface
create_ui_aspect <- function(component_name) {
  list(
    render = function(id) {
      # Return UI elements
    },
    validate = function() {
      # Validate UI structure
    }
  )
}

# Server aspect interface
create_server_aspect <- function(component_name) {
  list(
    initialize = function(id, data) {
      # Initialize server logic
    },
    update = function(new_data) {
      # Update reactive values
    }
  )
}

# Filter aspect interface
create_filter_aspect <- function(component_name) {
  list(
    apply = function(data, criteria) {
      # Apply filtering
    },
    reset = function() {
      # Reset filters
    }
  )
}
```

# Section 5: Testing Strategy {#testing-strategy}

## Subsection 1: Independent Aspect Testing {#independent-testing}

```r
# Test UI aspect (without Server)
test_that("UI components render correctly", {
  ui <- marketingChannelUI("test")
  expect_s3_class(ui, "shiny.tag.list")
  expect_true(grepl("selectInput", as.character(ui)))
})

# Test Server aspect (with mock UI)
test_that("Server logic handles inputs correctly", {
  testServer(marketingChannelServer, {
    session$setInputs(channel = "online")
    expect_equal(selected_channel(), "online")
  })
})

# Test Filter aspect (pure function testing)
test_that("Filter correctly filters data", {
  test_data <- data.frame(
    channel = c("online", "offline", "online"),
    value = c(100, 200, 300)
  )
  
  filtered <- marketingChannelFilters(test_data, "online")
  expect_equal(nrow(filtered), 2)
  expect_true(all(filtered$channel == "online"))
})
```

## Subsection 2: Integration Testing {#integration-testing}

```r
# Test aspect integration only when necessary
test_that("UI and Server aspects integrate correctly", {
  # Create test app
  ui <- fluidPage(
    marketingChannelUI("test")
  )
  
  server <- function(input, output, session) {
    marketingChannelServer("test", reactive(test_data))
  }
  
  # Run integration test
  app <- shinytest2::AppDriver$new(
    app = shinyApp(ui, server)
  )
  
  # Verify integrated behavior
  app$set_inputs(`test-channel` = "online")
  output <- app$get_value(output = "test-result")
  expect_equal(output$channel, "online")
})
```

# Section 6: Best Practices {#best-practices}

## Subsection 1: Conditional Loading {#conditional-loading}

```r
# Selectively load aspects based on application mode
load_app_aspects <- function(mode = "full") {
  base_path <- "components"
  
  if (mode == "full") {
    # Load all aspects
    load_aspect(base_path, "*Defaults.R")
    load_aspect(base_path, "*UI.R")
    load_aspect(base_path, "*Server.R")
    load_aspect(base_path, "*Filters.R")
    
  } else if (mode == "view_only") {
    # Read-only mode: only UI and basic Server
    load_aspect(base_path, "*UI.R")
    load_aspect(base_path, "*Server.R")
    
  } else if (mode == "api") {
    # API mode: only data processing
    load_aspect(base_path, "*Filters.R")
    load_aspect(base_path, "*Defaults.R")
  }
}

load_aspect <- function(path, pattern) {
  files <- list.files(
    path = path,
    pattern = pattern,
    recursive = TRUE,
    full.names = TRUE
  )
  lapply(files, source)
  invisible(files)
}
```

## Subsection 2: Dependency Management {#dependency-management}

```r
# Explicitly declare dependencies between aspects
aspect_dependencies <- list(
  UI = c(),                    # UI has no dependencies
  Defaults = c(),              # Defaults has no dependencies
  Server = c("UI", "Defaults"), # Server depends on UI and Defaults
  Filters = c("Defaults")      # Filters depends on Defaults
)

# Load in dependency order
load_with_dependencies <- function(aspect, loaded = character()) {
  if (aspect %in% loaded) return(loaded)
  
  # Load dependencies first
  deps <- aspect_dependencies[[aspect]]
  for (dep in deps) {
    loaded <- load_with_dependencies(dep, loaded)
  }
  
  # Load current aspect
  load_aspect("components", paste0("*", aspect, ".R"))
  c(loaded, aspect)
}
```

# Implementation Checklist {#checklist}

:::{.callout-note}
## N-tuple Independent Loading Checklist

**Loading Strategy**:
- [ ] Load by aspect not by component
- [ ] Loading order aligns with execution flow
- [ ] Support selective loading
- [ ] Dependencies explicit

**File Organization**:
- [ ] Clear directory structure
- [ ] Consistent naming conventions
- [ ] Aspect files properly separated
- [ ] Easy to locate specific aspects

**Test Coverage**:
- [ ] Each aspect tested independently
- [ ] Aspect interfaces tested
- [ ] Integration tests when necessary
- [ ] Test loading order

**Performance Optimization**:
- [ ] Avoid duplicate loading
- [ ] Support lazy loading
- [ ] Parallel loading possibility
- [ ] Memory efficiency

**Maintainability**:
- [ ] Clear aspect boundaries
- [ ] Complete documentation
- [ ] Easy to add aspects
- [ ] Easy to replace aspects
:::

# Conclusion {#conclusion}

The N-tuple Independent Loading Principle ensures flexibility and maintainability in component architecture. By organizing loading by aspect rather than by component, applications gain better modularity, testability, and performance. Remember, the power of the n-tuple pattern lies in the independence of aspects, and proper loading strategy is key to achieving this independence.

---

*Based on: Structure and Organization Law Article 13*  
*Related to: [UI_P004 Component N-tuple Pattern](../../CH04_ui_components/principles/UI_P004_component_ntuple_pattern.qmd), [MP017 Separation of Concerns](../../CH00_fundamental_principles/03_development_methodology/MP017_separation_of_concerns.qmd)*
